report_name: Attacks on Legacy Systems Hit 77% of UK Utility Companies, Report Finds

threat_actors.name: Adversaries Targeting UK Utilities Sector
threat_actors.description: Unspecified threat actors targeting UK utility companies by exploiting legacy OT systems with known vulnerabilities, as described in the Bridewell Cyber Security in Critical National Infrastructure Report 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exploit known, unpatched vulnerabilities in legacy OT software and hardware, which often cannot be easily patched.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Phishing is a common initial access vector used by attackers to compromise the IT network before pivoting to the OT environment.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) to pivot from a compromised IT network to the less-secure OT environment.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: The execution of PowerShell on Human-Machine Interface (HMI) workstations is considered a highly suspicious indicator of an attacker scripting interactions with control systems.

tools.name: Remote Desktop Protocol client
tools.description: The Remote Desktop Protocol client (mstsc.exe) is used to move laterally from corporate IT networks to operational technology (OT) networks.

identities.name: UK Utilities Sector
identities.description: A class representing utility companies in the United Kingdom, 77% of which were reported to have been targeted by cyber attacks on legacy OT systems in the past year.

indicators.name: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
id: 2b8a1c3d-f4e5-4b9a-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects RDP or SMB traffic originating from the corporate IT network and destined for the OT network, which could indicate an attacker pivoting from IT to OT. This rule requires site-specific tuning of the 'IT_Subnets' and 'OT_Subnets' lists.
references:
  - https://www.energylivenews.com/2026/06/18/staggering-77-of-utility-companies-hit-by-cyber-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1021.001
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.lateral_movement
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative access from designated jump servers or management workstations. Filter these out by source IP.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    src_ip|cidr:
      - 192.168.0.0/16 # Placeholder for IT_Subnets
      - 10.0.0.0/8     # Placeholder for IT_Subnets
    dst_ip|cidr:
      - 172.16.0.0/12  # Placeholder for OT_Subnets
    dst_port:
      - 3389 # RDP
      - 445  # SMB
  filter_jump_server:
    src_ip:
      - 10.1.1.1 # Placeholder for legitimate jump server IP
  condition: selection and not filter_jump_server
indicators.description: Detects RDP or SMB traffic originating from the corporate IT network and destined for the OT network, which could indicate an attacker pivoting from IT to OT.

indicators.name: PowerShell Execution on HMI Workstation
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Execution on HMI Workstation
id: 9e8f7d6c-5b4a-4c3d-2b1a-0e9f8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PowerShell on a Human-Machine Interface (HMI) workstation. This is highly suspicious and may indicate an attacker attempting to script interactions with the control system. The rule's effectiveness depends on correctly identifying HMI hostnames.
references:
  - https://www.energylivenews.com/2026/06/18/staggering-77-of-utility-companies-hit-by-cyber-attacks/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-18
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripting on HMIs, although this should be rare.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    ComputerName|contains:
      - 'HMI' # Placeholder for HMI naming convention
      - 'SCADA' # Placeholder for HMI naming convention
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PowerShell on a Human-Machine Interface (HMI) workstation, which is highly suspicious and may indicate attacker activity.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous IT to OT Network Traffic
